page: Microsoft Azure B2als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks highlight a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core performance scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show significant resource constraints. The stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively, reflecting the single physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth is also restricted, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 128.78 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 24.18 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving benchmarks remain in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B2als_v2 offers an economical entry point for workloads that do not require continuous CPU utilization. The burstable CPU allocation model allows users to pay a lower baseline rate while retaining the ability to handle occasional spikes in demand. This resource density profile makes the server suitable for low-traffic web servers, light background tasks, small-scale development or testing environments, and basic automation scripts that can tolerate variable CPU performance and limited memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure B2als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on a single physical core, this x86_64 server is designed for entry-level workloads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports premium storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations are highly constrained, placing them in the lower performance tiers. This server represents a cost-effective option for workloads with variable CPU demands, such as light web serving, basic automation, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It is designed for cost-effective, low-intensity workloads with variable CPU demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A low-cost, burstable virtual machine with strong single-core performance and a compact memory footprint for light workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-effective handling of variable workloads
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasted by constrained multi-core capability
No bundled local storage or GPU, but supports premium storage options
Best suited for light web serving, development environments, and basic automation
